steps.description: Select the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option in the dropdown menu within the "Create Declarative Agent" popup in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Default Folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Click the "Provision" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the left sidebar of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.

Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code – Insiders, shown while viewing the "Overview of the Declarative Agent template" README with the "Provision" step selected in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar.
Type 'notifications: Show Notifications' into the VS Code command palette input at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Expand Navigation" button located in the upper-left panel of the M365 Copilot Chat web interface.
Enter the text "how can you assistant me?" into the main chat input box of the "vscuse_app_XHhWMdev" agent within the M365 Copilot web interface.
Press Enter in the chat input box of the M365 Copilot web interface under the ${{var:app_name}} agent to submit the typed message.
